items.headline: Historic house to be preserved as four townhomes added behind
items.summary: A historic 960-square-foot single-family house on Crescent Avenue will be retained while four three-story townhomes are built in the rear. The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing the resource alteration permit to ensure the historic structure is appropriately protected during development.

items.headline: City Council amends digital signage agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: San José is modifying its contract with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signs on city-owned property. This could affect where and how digital advertising appears throughout the city.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for homeless encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council reviewed a request for proposals to hire contractors for encampment cleanup, indicating the city is formalizing how it handles removal and sanitation of homeless encampments.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: City Council approved actions related to replacing elevators at a terminal facility, likely affecting transit or transportation infrastructure residents use.

items.headline: City negotiating to buy Finch Avenue property from school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to purchase land at 10480 Finch Avenue. The outcome could affect what gets built on this currently school-controlled property.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area, which will guide future recreational amenities and green space development in this key commercial zone. This plan shapes how the district will balance growth with public access to outdoor spaces.

items.headline: Major 260-unit apartment complex approved for Berryessa
items.summary: The Berryessa Family Apartments, a $52.4 million new construction project, will add 260 residential units to the Berryessa neighborhood. This is one of the largest housing developments in recent permits and represents significant new housing supply in an area with transit access.

items.headline: Arc'Teryx flagship store opening at Stevens Creek
items.summary: The outdoor gear company Arc'Teryx is opening a major retail location at Stevens Creek Boulevard with a $1.7 million buildout. This signals growing retail investment in San José and represents a notable addition to the commercial landscape.
